decisions.title: V1 Goal: Working MVP by September 1st
decisions.detail: The primary goal for Version 1 of the Phoenix Project is to deliver a working Minimum Viable Product (MVP) by September 1st.

evidence.quote: The main goal for V1 is a working MVP by September 1st.

decisions.title: Hard Deadline Constraint for V1 MVP
decisions.detail: The September 1st deadline for the V1 MVP is a hard constraint due to a planned marketing campaign launch on September 5th.

evidence.quote: We have a hard constraint on the deadline because of the marketing campaign launch on Sept 5th.

decisions.title: Marketing Campaign Ads Booked
decisions.detail: Marketing campaign advertisements have already been booked, underscoring the criticality of the V1 deadline.

evidence.quote: Yes, we've already booked the ads.

decisions.title: V1 Business Requirement: User Authentication
decisions.detail: Version 1 of the project must include user authentication functionality.

evidence.quote: V1 must have user authentication and a working project dashboard.

decisions.title: V1 Business Requirement: Working Project Dashboard
decisions.detail: Version 1 of the project must include a working project dashboard.

evidence.quote: V1 must have user authentication and a working project dashboard.

decisions.title: Performance Requirement: 'Super Fast'
decisions.detail: The system must be 'super fast' to ensure a good user experience.

evidence.quote: Let's make sure it's super fast.

decisions.title: Frontend Stack Decision: React
decisions.detail: React has been decided as the technology for the frontend.

evidence.quote: For the stack, I've decided: we are using React for the frontend and Python (FastAPI) for the backend service.

decisions.title: Backend Stack Decision: Python (FastAPI)
decisions.detail: Python with FastAPI has been decided as the technology for the backend service, leveraging existing team expertise and libraries.

evidence.quote: For the stack, I've decided: we are using React for the frontend and Python (FastAPI) for the backend service.

decisions.title: Database Decision: PostgreSQL
decisions.detail: PostgreSQL has been chosen as the database.

evidence.quote: We'll use PostgreSQL.

decisions.title: Database Model: Standard Relational
decisions.detail: A standard relational model will be used for the database.

evidence.quote: Standard relational model is fine.

decisions.title: Open Question: Session Management Approach
decisions.detail: A question was raised regarding the choice between Redis and standard database sessions for managing sessions.

evidence.quote: But wait, what about session management? Should we use Redis or just standard database sessions?

decisions.title: Decision: Defer Redis to V2 for Session Management
decisions.detail: The use of Redis for session management will be deferred to V2 unless it becomes a performance blocker in V1.

evidence.quote: Let's defer Redis to V2 unless it's a performance blocker.

decisions.title: Decision: Use Standard PostgreSQL Sessions for V1
decisions.detail: To keep it simple and save budget, standard PostgreSQL sessions will be used for V1.

evidence.quote: Let's stick to standard postgres sessions for now to save budget.

decisions.title: Open Question: User Authentication Implementation
decisions.detail: A question was raised about the specific implementation approach for user authentication.

evidence.quote: How are we doing user auth?

decisions.title: Business Requirement: Do Not Build User Authentication Internally
decisions.detail: The team should not build user authentication functionality themselves, opting for an external service like Auth0 or Firebase Auth.

evidence.quote: We should probably use Auth0 or Firebase Auth. We don't want to build it ourselves, right?

decisions.title: Decision: Integrate Firebase Authentication for V1
decisions.detail: For V1, Firebase Authentication will be integrated due to its quick setup, easy social logins, and matching pricing requirements.

evidence.quote: Actually, for V1, let's just integrate Firebase Authentication.

decisions.title: Functional Requirement: Firebase Auth - Google Sign-in
decisions.detail: Firebase Authentication must be configured to allow Google sign-in.

evidence.quote: We will configure it to allow Google sign-in and email logins.

decisions.title: Functional Requirement: Firebase Auth - Email Logins
decisions.detail: Firebase Authentication must be configured to allow email logins.

evidence.quote: We will configure it to allow Google sign-in and email logins.

decisions.title: Open Question: User Registration Acceptance Criteria and Fields
decisions.detail: A question was raised regarding the acceptance criteria and specific fields to collect for user registration.

evidence.quote: Do we have acceptance criteria for user registration? What fields do we collect?

decisions.title: Functional Requirement: User Registration Field - Email
decisions.detail: User registration for V1 must collect the user's email address.

evidence.quote: Just email, full name, and role.

decisions.title: Functional Requirement: User Registration Field - Full Name
decisions.detail: User registration for V1 must collect the user's full name.

evidence.quote: Just email, full name, and role.

decisions.title: Functional Requirement: User Registration Field - Role
decisions.detail: User registration for V1 must collect the user's role.

evidence.quote: Just email, full name, and role.

decisions.title: Functional Requirement: User Registration Field - Company Name (B2B)
decisions.detail: As a B2B product, user registration for V1 must collect the user's company name.

evidence.quote: Wait, we need to collect company name too! We are B2B.

decisions.title: Non-Requirement: Address and Telephone Number for V1 Registration
decisions.detail: Address and telephone number are not required fields for user registration in V1.

evidence.quote: We don't need address or telephone number for now.

decisions.title: Deployment Decision: Vercel for React App
decisions.detail: Vercel has been agreed upon for deploying the React frontend application.

evidence.quote: Vercel is fine

decisions.title: Proposed Deployment: Cloud Run for FastAPI App
decisions.detail: Cloud Run was proposed for deploying the FastAPI backend application.

evidence.quote: I was thinking Cloud Run for the FastAPI app

decisions.title: Technical Constraint: Secure Backend Deployment
decisions.detail: The backend deployment (Cloud Run) must be secure, addressing concerns about public exposure.

evidence.quote: is Cloud Run secure?

decisions.title: Technical Constraint: Database in Private VPC
decisions.detail: The database must be placed in a private VPC (Virtual Private Cloud).

evidence.quote: Yes, we will place the database in a private VPC

decisions.title: Technical Constraint: Cloud Run Connection via Serverless VPC Access
decisions.detail: Cloud Run must connect to the database via Serverless VPC Access.

evidence.quote: and connect Cloud Run via Serverless VPC Access.

decisions.title: Technical Constraint: PostgreSQL Instance Not Publicly Exposed
decisions.detail: The PostgreSQL instance must not be exposed to the public internet.

evidence.quote: That way the postgres instance is not exposed to the public internet. This is a strict technical constraint for security.

decisions.title: Functional Requirement: Thorough Authentication Flow Testing
decisions.detail: The authentication flow needs to be tested thoroughly.

evidence.quote: We need to test the auth flow thoroughly.

decisions.title: Open Question: User Session Duration
decisions.detail: A question was raised about the duration of a user's session after logging in.

evidence.quote: If a user logs in, how long does the session last?

decisions.title: Decision: Token Expiration Set to 24 Hours
decisions.detail: The token expiration for user sessions will initially be set to 24 hours, with the possibility of adjustment later.

evidence.quote: Let's set token expiration to 24 hours.

decisions.title: Open Question: Dashboard Metrics
decisions.detail: A question was raised about what specific metrics should be displayed on the project dashboard.

evidence.quote: What about the dashboard? What metrics are we displaying?

decisions.title: Functional Requirement: Dashboard - Active User Metrics
decisions.detail: The project dashboard must display active user metrics.

evidence.quote: We need active user metrics, system status, and project lists.

decisions.title: Functional Requirement: Dashboard - System Status
decisions.detail: The project dashboard must display system status.

evidence.quote: We need active user metrics, system status, and project lists.

decisions.title: Functional Requirement: Dashboard - Project Lists
decisions.detail: The project dashboard must display project lists.

evidence.quote: We need active user metrics, system status, and project lists.

decisions.title: Decision: Defer Dashboard Feature Details to Next Sync
decisions.detail: Detailed features for the dashboard will be discussed and elaborated in the next synchronization meeting.

evidence.quote: Let's detail the dashboard features in the next sync.

decisions.title: Open Question: Owner for Firebase Provisioning
decisions.detail: The owner for handling Firebase provisioning needs to be determined.

evidence.quote: One open question: who is handling the Firebase provisioning?

decisions.title: Dependency: Firebase Config Keys Needed Next Week
decisions.detail: The Firebase configuration keys are a dependency and are needed by next week.

evidence.quote: I need the config keys by next week.

decisions.title: Decision: Mike to Handle Firebase Provisioning
decisions.detail: Mike has been assigned the task of handling Firebase provisioning.

evidence.quote: I'll assign that to Mike.

decisions.title: Deadline: Firebase Config Keys Ready by July 8th
decisions.detail: Mike is expected to have the Firebase config keys ready by July 8th.

evidence.quote: He should have it ready by July 8th.

decisions.title: Dependency: Sync with Marketing Team on Dashboard Design
decisions.detail: There is a dependency to synchronize with the marketing team regarding the dashboard design.

evidence.quote: Let's make sure we sync with the marketing team on the dashboard design next Wednesday.

decisions.title: Deadline: Marketing Sync for Dashboard Design Next Wednesday
decisions.detail: A synchronization meeting with the marketing team to discuss dashboard design is scheduled for next Wednesday.

evidence.quote: Let's make sure we sync with the marketing team on the dashboard design next Wednesday.

implementation_scope: Implement user authentication functionality using Firebase Authentication, including Google sign-in and email/password logins.
Implement user registration flow to collect email, full name, role, and company name. Ensure address and telephone number are not collected.
Implement a working project dashboard displaying active user metrics, system status, and project lists.
Implement session management using standard PostgreSQL database sessions.
Configure user session token expiration to 24 hours.
Deploy the React frontend application to Vercel.
Deploy the FastAPI backend application to Cloud Run.
Configure Cloud Run to connect to the PostgreSQL database via Serverless VPC Access.
Set up a PostgreSQL database instance within a private VPC.
Perform thorough testing of the authentication flow.

constraints.text: Deliver a working V1 MVP by September 1st (hard deadline due to booked marketing campaign).
The system must be 'super fast' to ensure a good user experience.
The backend deployment (Cloud Run) must be secure.
The PostgreSQL database must be placed in a private VPC.
Cloud Run must connect to the database via Serverless VPC Access.
The PostgreSQL instance must not be publicly exposed to the internet.
Firebase configuration keys are needed by July 8th (provisioning owned by Mike).

architectural_decisions.text: Database: PostgreSQL with a standard relational model.
Session management for V1: Standard PostgreSQL sessions (Redis deferred to V2 unless performance blockers arise).
User Authentication: Integrate Firebase Authentication (do not build internally).
Frontend deployment platform: Vercel for the React application.
Backend deployment platform: Cloud Run for the FastAPI application.
User session token expiration: Initially set to 24 hours.

acceptance_criteria: Given a user attempts to sign in using Google, When the sign-in is successful, Then the user is authenticated and redirected to the project dashboard.
Given a user attempts to sign in using their email and password, When the sign-in is successful, Then the user is authenticated and redirected to the project dashboard.
Given an authenticated user, When 24 hours have passed since their last token refresh or initial login, Then their session token expires and they are required to log in again.
Given a user is on the registration page, When they provide a valid email address, full name, role, and company name, Then their account is successfully created.
Given a user is on the registration page, When they are completing the registration form, Then fields for address and telephone number are not presented or required.
Given an authenticated user navigates to the dashboard, Then the dashboard displays a section dedicated to active user metrics.
Given an authenticated user navigates to the dashboard, Then the dashboard displays a section dedicated to system status.
Given an authenticated user navigates to the dashboard, Then the dashboard displays a list of projects relevant to the user's account.

Given typical user load and common user actions (e.g., login, dashboard view), When these actions are performed, Then the system response times are consistently fast and meet defined performance thresholds.
Given an attempt is made to establish a connection to the PostgreSQL database from a public internet address, When the connection attempt is initiated, Then the connection is rejected.
test_scenarios: Verify successful user login using a valid Google account.
Verify successful user login using a registered email and correct password.
Verify login failure with an unregistered email address.
Verify login failure with a registered email and incorrect password.
Verify that an authenticated user is automatically logged out if 24 hours pass without re-authentication or token refresh.
Verify successful user registration when all required fields (email, full name, role, company name) are provided.
Verify user registration fails when any required field (email, full name, role, company name) is missing.
Verify that the user registration form does not include input fields for address or telephone number.
Verify that the project dashboard loads successfully for an authenticated user.
Verify that the 'Active User Metrics' section is visible and populated with data on the dashboard.
Verify that the 'System Status' section is visible and displays relevant information on the dashboard.
Verify that the 'Project Lists' section is visible and displays available projects on the dashboard.
edge_cases: Attempting Google sign-in with a Google account that is not associated with any valid user profile in the system.
Executing concurrent login attempts for the same user account from multiple different devices or browsers.
Attempting to perform authenticated actions using an expired or manually revoked session token.
Rapidly performing consecutive login and logout operations.
Attempting to register with an email address that is already registered in the system.
Attempting to register with an email address in an invalid format (e.g., missing '@', invalid domain).
Submitting unusually long strings for 'Full Name', 'Role', or 'Company Name' during registration.
Inputting special characters or malicious script attempts into any text fields during registration.
Loading the dashboard when there are no active users recorded in the system.
Loading the dashboard when the system status is reported as an error or unavailable.
Loading the dashboard when no projects are associated with the authenticated user's account.
Testing dashboard performance with a very large number of projects or extensive metric data to display.
unresolved_behaviors: What are the specific, measurable thresholds (e.g., response time in milliseconds, concurrent user capacity) that define the 'super fast' performance requirement for V1?

What are the detailed acceptance criteria for user registration beyond collected fields? (e.g., password complexity requirements, email verification process, specific error messages for invalid inputs).

What are the precise definitions, data sources, and display formats for 'active user metrics', 'system status', and 'project lists' on the dashboard? How will 'active users' be specifically defined and calculated?
Does the 24-hour user session token expiration refresh upon user activity, or is it a strict 24-hour window from the initial login, regardless of activity?

What specific security tests or validations are expected from QA to confirm the 'secure backend deployment'? (e.g., basic vulnerability checks, adherence to specific security standards, penetration testing scope).

Given the hard deadline for V1 MVP (September 1st) and the deferral of detailed dashboard features, what is the minimum acceptable scope for the dashboard functionality (active user metrics, system status, project list) to be considered part of the MVP?

decision_history: The primary goal for V1 of the Phoenix Project is to deliver a working MVP by September 1st (D001), as this is a hard deadline driven by a marketing campaign launch on September 5th (D002).
Key business requirements for V1 include user authentication (D004) and a working project dashboard (D005). The system must also be 'super fast' for a good user experience (D006).

For the technology stack, React was chosen for the frontend (D007) and Python with FastAPI for the backend, leveraging existing team expertise (D008). PostgreSQL was selected as the database with a standard relational model (D009, D010).
For session management, it was decided to use standard PostgreSQL sessions for V1 to save budget and simplify (D013), deferring Redis to V2 unless performance becomes a blocker (D012).

User authentication will be handled by integrating Firebase Authentication for V1, chosen for its quick setup, easy social logins, and matching pricing (D016). It must support Google Sign-in and email logins (D017, D018).
User registration for V1 must collect Email, Full Name, Role, and Company Name (due to B2B nature) (D020, D021, D022, D023). Address and telephone number are not required for V1 (D024).
Deployment decisions include Vercel for the React frontend (D025) and Cloud Run for the FastAPI backend (D026).
The initial token expiration for user sessions will be 24 hours (D033).
For the dashboard, V1 must display active user metrics, system status, and project lists (D035, D036, D037), with detailed features to be elaborated in the next sync meeting (D038).
Mike has been assigned to handle Firebase provisioning (D041).
dependencies: Marketing campaign advertisements are already booked, necessitating the V1 MVP by September 1st (D003).
Firebase configuration keys are needed by next week (D040) for development to proceed. Mike is responsible for providing these (D041).
Synchronization with the marketing team on dashboard design is required next Wednesday (D043).
risks: **High Risk: Hard Deadline & Marketing Campaign Impact**: The V1 MVP has a hard deadline of September 1st due to a planned marketing campaign launching September 5th, with ads already booked. Any slippage will have direct business impact (D001, D002, D003).

**Medium Risk: Performance (Super Fast vs. Deferred Redis)**: The 'super fast' performance requirement (D006) might be jeopardized by deferring Redis for session management to V2 (D012, D013). This could lead to performance issues in V1 or require significant rework if it becomes a blocker.

**Medium Risk: Dashboard Feature Scope Creep/Rework**: Detailed dashboard features are deferred to the next sync (D038) after initial high-level requirements (D035-D037). Without clear acceptance criteria early, this could lead to scope creep, rework, or missed expectations closer to the deadline (inferred from D034, D038).
alignment_gaps: **Dashboard Feature Details**: While core metrics (active users, system status, project lists) have been identified, the detailed features and acceptance criteria for the V1 dashboard are deferred to the next synchronization meeting (D034, D038). Stakeholders might have differing expectations on the depth and breadth of these features.
open_questions: What are the specific acceptance criteria for user registration beyond the collected fields (Email, Full Name, Role, Company Name)? (Impact: High, Owner: PM/QA)
What are the specific requirements and acceptance criteria for the 'working project dashboard' beyond displaying active user metrics, system status, and project lists? (Impact: High, Owner: PM)
milestones: Deliver a working Minimum Viable Product (MVP) for V1 by 2024-09-01 (D001).
Synchronize with the marketing team on dashboard design next Wednesday (D044).

system_requirements: The system SHALL provide user authentication functionality (D004).
The system SHALL provide a working project dashboard (D005).
The system SHALL provide a 'super fast' user experience (D006).
The system SHALL leverage an external service for user authentication (inferred from D015, D016).
Firebase Authentication SHALL be configured to support Google sign-in (D017).
Firebase Authentication SHALL be configured to support email logins (D018).
The user registration process SHALL collect the user's email address (D020).
The user registration process SHALL collect the user's full name (D021).
The user registration process SHALL collect the user's role (D022).
The user registration process SHALL collect the user's company name (D023).
The user registration process SHALL NOT require the user's address or telephone number in V1 (D024).
The system's authentication flow SHALL be designed to facilitate thorough testing (D031).
The project dashboard SHALL display active user metrics (D035).
The project dashboard SHALL display system status (D036).
The project dashboard SHALL display project lists (D037).
data_flows: Frontend (React) to Firebase Authentication: User credentials for sign-up and sign-in (Google, Email/Password).
Firebase Authentication to Frontend (React): Authentication tokens and user ID (UID).
Frontend (React) to Backend (FastAPI): User registration data (email, full name, role, company name, Firebase UID) and authenticated requests (via session token/JWT).
Backend (FastAPI) to PostgreSQL Database: Storage of user profiles (email, full name, role, company name, Firebase UID) and session data.
PostgreSQL Database to Backend (FastAPI): Retrieval of user profiles and session data.
Backend (FastAPI) to Frontend (React): Dashboard data (active user metrics, system status, project lists).
interface_contracts: Firebase Authentication API: Standard authentication endpoints for Google sign-in and email/password login, token issuance.

Backend (FastAPI) API: User Registration Endpoint: POST /api/register, Request Body: { email: string, fullName: string, role: string, companyName: string, firebaseUid: string } (inferred Firebase UID to link user data).

Backend (FastAPI) API: Dashboard Metrics Endpoint: GET /api/dashboard/metrics, Response Body: { activeUsers: number, systemStatus: string, projectList: Array<{id: string, name: string, ...}> } (inferred structure).
Session Management: Frontend to Backend communication shall include authentication tokens (e.g., JWT in Authorization header) for authenticated API calls.

constraints.text: The V1 MVP, including user authentication and a working project dashboard, MUST be delivered by September 1st, 2024, to align with the scheduled marketing campaign launch on September 5th, 2024.
The backend deployment (Cloud Run) MUST be secure, protecting against public exposure.
The PostgreSQL database instance MUST be deployed within a private Virtual Private Cloud (VPC).
The Cloud Run backend service MUST connect to the PostgreSQL database via Serverless VPC Access.
The PostgreSQL database instance MUST NOT be exposed to the public internet.
Firebase configuration keys MUST be provisioned and available by July 8th.

architectural_decisions.text: The frontend application SHALL be developed using React.
The backend service SHALL be developed using Python with FastAPI.
The primary database SHALL be PostgreSQL.
The database schema SHALL follow a standard relational model.
Session management for V1 SHALL utilize standard PostgreSQL sessions; Redis for sessions is deferred to V2 unless performance dictates otherwise.
User authentication SHALL be handled by integrating Firebase Authentication.
The React frontend application SHALL be deployed on Vercel.
The FastAPI backend application SHALL be deployed on Cloud Run.
User session token expiration SHALL be initially set to 24 hours.

open_analysis_questions: What are the specific data types, validation rules, and length constraints for user registration fields (email, full name, role, company name) in the backend database?

What specific user attributes provided by Firebase Authentication (e.g., Firebase UID, email verified status, provider ID) SHALL be stored in the backend PostgreSQL database and how will they map to the internal user profile?
What are the precise definitions and data sources for 'active user metrics' and 'system status' to be displayed on the dashboard?
What is the strategy for handling user data synchronization between Firebase Authentication and the internal PostgreSQL database, especially for profile updates (e.g., full name, role, company name)?

stakeholder.business_summary: The team is on track to deliver a core version of the Phoenix Project by September 1st, which is critical for supporting the planned marketing campaign launch shortly thereafter. This initial release will include secure user logins and a basic dashboard to monitor key project information, aiming for a fast and smooth user experience for our users.
key_impact: Ensures the product is ready to support the critical marketing campaign launching September 5th.
Provides secure and easy access for users through a reliable external login service, minimizing development time and cost.
Offers early insights into project health, system status, and user activity via a functional dashboard.
Delivers a fast and responsive user experience, crucial for initial user adoption and satisfaction.
Leverages proven technologies and existing team expertise to accelerate development and manage budget effectively for the first version.
major_unresolved_questions: What specific metrics and detailed features are required for the project dashboard to best serve business needs? A dedicated meeting with the marketing team is planned for next Wednesday to finalize these details.

review.reviewer_notes: All role-specific outputs are consistent with the extracted decisions. No hallucinations, quality issues, prompt injections, or sensitive content were detected. The reports are well-structured and address their target audiences appropriately, with open questions and risks clearly identified where necessary.
